slices.name: report_warning
slices.code:
92 | def report_warning(message: str) -> None:
  93 |     print(f"[WARN] {message}", file=sys.stderr)

slices.name: normalize_extensions
slices.code:
96 | def normalize_extensions(values: List[str]) -> List[str]:
  97 |     normalized = []
  98 |     for item in values:
  99 |         trimmed = item.strip()
 100 |         if not trimmed:
 101 |             continue
 102 |         if not trimmed.startswith("."):
 103 |             trimmed = "." + trimmed
 104 |         normalized.append(trimmed.lower())
 105 |     return normalized

slices.name: sha1_file
slices.code:
108 | def sha1_file(path: Path, chunk: int = 1024 * 1024) -> str:
 109 |     h = hashlib.sha1()
 110 |     try:
 111 |         with path.open("rb") as handle:
 112 |             while True:
 113 |                 chunk_bytes = handle.read(chunk)
 114 |                 if not chunk_bytes:
 115 |                     break
 116 |                 h.update(chunk_bytes)
 117 |         return h.hexdigest()
 118 |     except PermissionError:
 119 |         return ""

slices.name: should_skip_dir
slices.code:
122 | def should_skip_dir(name: str, excludes: List[str]) -> bool:
 123 |     return any(name.lower() == candidate.lower() for candidate in excludes)

slices.name: is_suspicious_path
slices.code:
126 | def is_suspicious_path(abs_path: str, suspicious_dirs: List[str]) -> bool:
 127 |     normalized = abs_path.replace("\\", "/").lower()
 128 |     return any(
 129 |         normalized.endswith(f"/{entry.lower()}") or f"/{entry.lower()}/" in normalized
 130 |         for entry in suspicious_dirs
 131 |     )

slices.name: parse_list
slices.code:
134 | def parse_list(value: Optional[str], default: List[str]) -> List[str]:
 135 |     if value is None:
 136 |         return default
 137 |     return normalize_extensions([item.strip() for item in value.split(",") if item.strip()])

slices.name: parse_dirs
slices.code:
140 | def parse_dirs(value: Optional[str], default: List[str]) -> List[str]:
 141 |     if value is None:
 142 |         return default
 143 |     return [item.strip() for item in value.split(",") if item.strip()]

slices.name: get_profile
slices.code:
146 | def get_profile(name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 147 |     if name not in BUILTIN_PROFILES:
 148 |         raise ValueError(f"Unknown profile: {name}")
 149 |     return BUILTIN_PROFILES[name]

slices.name: scan_root
slices.code:
152 | def scan_root(
 153 |     root: Path,
 154 |     include_exts: List[str],
 155 |     excludes: List[str],
 156 |     do_hash: bool,
 157 |     max_file_size: int,
 158 |     suspicious_dirs: List[str],
 159 |     stats: Dict[str, Any],
 160 | ) -> Iterable[Dict[str, Any]]:
 161 |     root = root.resolve()
 162 |     print(f"Scanning root: {root}")
 163 | 
 164 |     for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
 165 |         dirnames[:] = [d for d in dirnames if not should_skip_dir(d, excludes)]
 166 | 
 167 |         for fn in filenames:
 168 |             p = Path(dirpath) / fn
 169 |             ext = p.suffix.lower()
 170 |             if include_exts and ext not in include_exts:
 171 |                 stats["skipped_by_ext"][ext] = stats["skipped_by_ext"].get(ext, 0) + 1
 172 |                 stats["skipped"] += 1
 173 |                 continue
 174 | 
 175 |             try:
 176 |                 stat = p.stat()
 177 |             except Exception:
 178 |                 stats["errors"] += 1
 179 |                 continue
 180 | 
 181 |             if max_file_size and stat.st_size > max_file_size:
 182 |                 stats["skipped"] += 1
 183 |                 stats["skipped_by_ext"][ext] = stats["skipped_by_ext"].get(ext, 0) + 1
 184 |                 stats["skipped_details"]["oversize"] += 1
 185 |                 continue
 186 | 
 187 |             record = {
 188 |                 "root": str(root),
 189 |                 "rel_path": str(p.relative_to(root)),
 190 |                 "abs_path": str(p),
 191 |                 "ext": ext,
 192 |                 "size": stat.st_size,
 193 |                 "mtime_iso": datetime.fromtimestamp(stat.st_mtime).isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
 194 |             }
 195 | 
 196 |             if do_hash and stat.st_size <= DEFAULT_HASH_SIZE_LIMIT:
 197 |                 record["sha1"] = sha1_file(p)
 198 |             else:
 199 |                 record["sha1"] = ""
 200 | 
 201 |             stats["bundled"] += 1
 202 |             if is_suspicious_path(str(p), suspicious_dirs):
 203 |                 stats["polluted_files"].append(str(p))
 204 |             yield record

slices.name: build_health_report
slices.code:
207 | def build_health_report(
 208 |     roots: List[str],
 209 |     stats: Dict[str, Any],
 210 |     profile_name: str,
 211 |     profile_settings: Dict[str, Any],
 212 |     out_path: str,
 213 |     fail_on_pollution: bool,
 214 | ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 215 |     report = {
 216 |         "profile": profile_name,
 217 |         "output": out_path,
 218 |         "roots": roots,
 219 |         "row_count": stats["bundled"],
 220 |         "skipped": stats["skipped"],
 221 |         "errors": stats["errors"],
 222 |         "skipped_details": stats["skipped_details"],
 223 |         "skipped_by_ext": stats["skipped_by_ext"],
 224 |         "polluted_files": stats["polluted_files"],
 225 |         "pollution_count": len(stats["polluted_files"]),
 226 |         "profile_settings": profile_settings,
 227 |         "max_file_size": profile_settings.get("max_file_size", 0),
 228 |         "fail_on_pollution": fail_on_pollution,
 229 |     }
 230 |     if report["pollution_count"] > 0:
 231 |         report["status"] = "WARN" if not fail_on_pollution else "BLOCK"
 232 |     else:
 233 |         report["status"] = "PASS"
 234 |     return report

slices.name: parse_args
slices.code:
237 | def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
 238 |     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Create a unified file map CSV.")
 239 |     parser.add_argument("--roots", nargs="+", help="Directory roots to scan. Defaults to current directory.")
 240 |     parser.add_argument("-o", "--out", help="CSV path to write. Defaults to 'file_map.csv'.")
 241 |     parser.add_argument("--include-exts", default=None, help="Comma-separated extensions.")
 242 |     parser.add_argument("--exclude-dirs", default=None, help="Comma-separated directories to exclude.")
 243 |     parser.add_argument("--hash", action="store_true", help="Compute SHA1 for files <=50MB.")
 244 |     parser.add_argument("--profile", default="default", help="Built-in scan profile to use.")
 245 |     parser.add_argument("--health-report", help="Optional JSON health report path.")
 246 |     parser.add_argument("--max-file-size", type=int, default=DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_SIZE, help="Maximum file size in bytes to include. 0 means no limit.")
 247 |     parser.add_argument("--fail-on-pollution", action="store_true", help="Exit non-zero if suspicious paths are detected.")
 248 |     return parser.parse_args()

slices.name: main
slices.code:
251 | def main() -> int:
 252 |     args = parse_args()
 253 |     if "-o" in sys.argv and "--out" not in sys.argv:
 254 |         report_warning("Using -o as an alias for --out. Prefer --out for compatibility.")
 255 | 
 256 |     roots_input = args.roots if args.roots else ["."]
 257 |     out_path = args.out if args.out else "file_map.csv"
 258 | 
 259 |     try:
 260 |         profile_settings = get_profile(args.profile)
 261 |     except ValueError as exc:
 262 |         print(f"Error: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
 263 |         return 1
 264 | 
 265 |     include_exts = parse_list(args.include_exts, profile_settings["include_exts"])
 266 |     exclude_dirs = parse_dirs(args.exclude_dirs, profile_settings["exclude_dirs"])
 267 |     max_file_size = args.max_file_size or profile_settings.get("max_file_size", DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_SIZE)
 268 |     suspicious_dirs = profile_settings.get("suspicious_dirs", DEFAULT_SUSPICIOUS_DIRECTORIES)
 269 | 
 270 |     rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 271 |     stats: Dict[str, Any] = {
 272 |         "bundled": 0,
 273 |         "skipped": 0,
 274 |         "errors": 0,
 275 |         "skipped_details": {"oversize": 0},
 276 |         "skipped_by_ext": {},
 277 |         "polluted_files": [],
 278 |     }
 279 | 
 280 |     print("Starting scan...")
 281 |     print(f"Target Output: {Path(out_path).resolve()}")
 282 | 
 283 |     for root_value in roots_input:
 284 |         root = Path(root_value).expanduser()
 285 |         if not root.exists():
 286 |             report_warning(f"Root not found: {root}")
 287 |             continue
 288 |         for rec in scan_root(root, include_exts, exclude_dirs, args.hash, max_file_size, suspicious_dirs, stats):
 289 |             rows.append(rec)
 290 | 
 291 |     out_p = Path(out_path)
 292 |     if out_p.parent.name:
 293 |         out_p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 294 | 
 295 |     try:
 296 |         with out_p.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
 297 |             writer = csv.DictWriter(handle, fieldnames=CSV_FIELDNAMES)
 298 |             writer.writeheader()
 299 |             for row in rows:
 300 |                 writer.writerow(row)
 301 |     except PermissionError:
 302 |         print(f"[ERROR] Could not write to {out_path}. Is the file open in Excel?", file=sys.stderr)
 303 |         return 1
 304 | 
 305 |     if args.health_report:
 306 |         health_report = build_health_report(
 307 |             roots_input,
 308 |             stats,
 309 |             args.profile,
 310 |             {
 311 |                 "include_exts": include_exts,
 312 |                 "exclude_dirs": exclude_dirs,
 313 |                 "max_file_size": max_file_size,
 314 |                 "suspicious_dirs": suspicious_dirs,
 315 |             },
 316 |             out_path,
 317 |             args.fail_on_pollution,
 318 |         )
 319 |         write_json_report(health_report, Path(args.health_report))
 320 |         print(f"Health report written to: {args.health_report}")
 321 | 
 322 |     print(f"Success! Wrote {len(rows)} rows to {out_path}")
 323 |     if stats["polluted_files"]:
 324 |         report_warning(f"Detected {len(stats['polluted_files'])} suspicious path(s) in the manifest.")
 325 | 
 326 |     if args.fail_on_pollution and stats["polluted_files"]:
 327 |         return 2
 328 |     return 0
